title: Single-Currency MVP — No Currency Column on Transaction
context: The brainstorm listed multi-currency support as in-scope. However, adding currency conversion requires an exchange rate source, complex UI, and significantly more data model complexity. BR-005 explicitly defers multi-currency to post-MVP.
decision: For the MVP, the app operates in a single implicit default currency. There is NO currency field on the Transaction or Account tables. All amounts are stored as plain REAL values in the same implicit currency. When multi-currency is added post-MVP, a migration will add a currency_code column to both accounts and transactions, and a currencies table with exchange rates.

alternatives.name: Add currency_code column now (even if unused)
alternatives.description: Include currency fields in the schema from day one, defaulting to a single currency.
cons: Increases schema complexity before it provides any value
Risk of UI showing an irrelevant currency picker, confusing users
Exchange rate logic must be thoughtfully designed — premature addition risks a bad data model

positive: Simpler schema and UI for MVP — no currency picker, no exchange rate concerns
Multi-currency can be designed correctly once the core app is proven
negative: A Room schema migration (adding currency_code columns) will be required when multi-currency is added
Existing data rows will need DEFAULT currency values during migration
neutral: Room's migration framework handles additive column migrations cleanly
